name: Market Chameleon
description_short: Market Chameleon is a specialized stock and options analytics platform with advanced IV rankings, earnings strategy backtesting, unusual options flow screening, and historical data back to 2014 for active traders.
description_long:
Market Chameleon is a serious options analytics platform — and we mean serious. Founded in 2006 in New York, this is one of the most data-rich tools available to retail options traders. Where other platforms give you surface-level options data, Market Chameleon gives you the full depth: historical implied volatility back to 2014, earnings move backtesting across 12+ releases, block trade detection, unusual flow screening, and strategy-level backtesting for multi-leg positions. It's the kind of tool that separates recreational options traders from systematic ones.

## The Company Behind It

Market Chameleon was founded in 2007 by William McBride in Philadelphia. That's nearly two decades of building options analytics — long before options trading became mainstream with retail traders. This longevity shows in the data depth: they've been collecting and structuring options data since well before most competitors existed, which is why their historical dataset goes back to 2014 for options and 1998 for stocks. It's a private, bootstrapped company that has grown through product quality rather than marketing spend.

Market Chameleon is also one of very few platforms that offers true options screeners — the ability to filter and scan the entire options market by IV rank, probability, strategy type, and historical performance. Most platforms give you an options chain for a single stock. Market Chameleon lets you screen across the entire market to find the best setups, which is a fundamentally different and more powerful approach to options trading.

## Implied Volatility Analytics — The Foundation

Market Chameleon's IV tools are arguably the deepest in the retail space. You get IV rank, IV percentile, historical IV charts, IV skew analysis, and term structure visualization across expirations. This matters because implied volatility is the single most important factor in options pricing — if you're trading options without understanding where IV sits relative to its historical range, you're flying blind.

The platform lets you screen for stocks with elevated or depressed IV, compare current IV to historical averages, and identify volatility crush or expansion setups. For premium sellers, this is essential — you want to sell when IV is high (expensive premiums) and buy when IV is low. Market Chameleon makes this analysis trivial.

## Earnings Strategy Backtesting — The Killer Feature

This is where Market Chameleon truly has no competition. The Earnings Trader plan lets you backtest specific options strategies (straddles, strangles, iron condors, verticals) across up to 12 past earnings releases for any stock. You can see exactly what would have happened if you'd sold an iron condor before AAPL's last 12 earnings reports — win rate, average return, max drawdown, and how the actual move compared to the implied move.

Why does this matter? Because earnings trades are the highest-edge options setups available to retail traders. The market consistently overprices implied moves around earnings (the 'volatility risk premium'), and Market Chameleon gives you the historical data to exploit this systematically. Instead of guessing whether a strangle sale will work, you can see the actual backtest results across multiple cycles.

No other retail platform offers this level of earnings-specific backtesting. ThinkOrSwim, TradingView, and OptionStrat all have great strategy tools, but none of them let you backtest options trades across historical earnings events the way Market Chameleon does.

## Options Flow & Unusual Activity

The Options Trader plan includes unusual options volume screening, block trade detection, and repeat trade screening. You can filter by volume-to-open-interest ratios, trade size, sentiment (bullish vs bearish flow), and spot institutional positioning before it moves the underlying. The repeat trade screener is particularly useful — it identifies when the same strategy is being executed repeatedly on the same underlying, which often signals institutional accumulation.

## Filtering for Options Selling Strategies

One feature that doesn't get enough attention is how well Market Chameleon handles filtering for specific options selling strategies. If you're a premium seller running iron condors, credit spreads, or cash-secured puts, you can filter for setups based on IV rank, probability of profit, expected return, days to expiration, and historical win rates. Instead of manually scanning chains and doing the math yourself, the platform surfaces the highest-probability selling opportunities across the entire market.

This is especially powerful combined with the earnings backtesting — you can identify stocks where selling premium before earnings has historically been profitable, filter by strategy type, and see exactly what the edge looks like over 12+ cycles. For systematic options sellers, this combination of screening and backtesting is hard to find anywhere else at this price point.

## Multi-Leg Strategy Backtesting

Beyond earnings, Market Chameleon lets you backtest multi-leg strategies (iron condors, verticals, butterflies, calendars) against real historical options data. This is backtesting with actual options prices, not theoretical models — a critical distinction. You can filter by delta, days to expiration, IV level at entry, and see how strategies performed across different market conditions.

## Stock Analytics

The stock-side tools are solid too. Premarket and postmarket analytics with VWAP, volume breakdowns, and trades-per-minute data. A stock screener covering sector, market cap, fundamentals, and 52-week range. Dividend forecasts. Stock data goes back to 1998, which is useful for long-term technical and fundamental analysis.

## Interactive Brokers Integration

Market Chameleon is available as a third-party research subscription through Interactive Brokers, which means IBKR users can access it directly within their brokerage platform. This is a nice convenience if you're already an IBKR client.

## Pricing — Honest Assessment

The pricing structure can be confusing because options and earnings tools are split across separate plans:

- **Free:** Limited data, delayed quotes, basic screening
- **Stock Trader ($39/mo):** All stock tools, premarket/postmarket, historical stock data
- **Options Trader ($69/mo):** Stock tools + options flow, IV analytics, multi-leg backtesting
- **Earnings Trader ($79/mo):** Stock tools + earnings backtesting (but NOT the options flow tools)
- **Total Access ($99/mo):** Everything combined

The catch: if you want both options flow AND earnings backtesting, you need Total Access at $99/mo. The individual plans force you to choose one or the other. For serious options traders, Total Access is the only plan that makes sense — and at $99/mo it's competitive with similar data platforms. All plans come with a 7-day free trial.

## What Could Be Better

Market Chameleon is browser-only — there's no mobile app. For a data-heavy analytics platform this isn't a dealbreaker (you're doing real analysis, not quick checks), but it means you can't get push notifications or check IV levels from your phone the way you can with OptionStrat.

The interface is dense. There's a lot of data on every page, and new users can feel overwhelmed. It takes a few sessions to learn where everything is and how to navigate efficiently. This isn't a platform you'll master in 30 minutes — but the depth of data rewards the learning curve.

Annual pricing isn't prominently displayed, which makes it harder to compare the long-term cost.

## Bottom Line

Market Chameleon sits at an interesting intersection — it's built for serious options traders who need advanced, institutional-grade data, but it's also one of the best learning platforms for newer traders who want to genuinely understand how options work. The depth of the data forces you to learn concepts like IV rank, term structure, and probability of profit in a practical context rather than from a textbook. You're not just reading about implied volatility — you're seeing it across 10 years of real data for every stock.

For experienced traders, Market Chameleon is the best options analytics platform for earnings trades and systematic premium selling. The backtesting capability alone is worth the subscription — there is literally nothing else like it in the retail space. For newer traders, the free tier and 7-day trials let you explore the data and start building a real understanding of options mechanics before committing. Either way, it's a really good piece of software that takes options analysis seriously, and it shows in every feature.
